applied_optimizations.name: torch.compile on Conv3d
applied_optimizations.description: Applied torch.compile(mode='default') to the Conv3d layer, which uses Triton conv backend on ROCm. This reduced conv time from ~2.56ms (MIOpen) to ~1.21ms (Triton). Also used a simple Triton kernel for bias add + sum.

attempted_but_failed.name: Fused Triton kernel for all post-conv ops
attempted_but_failed.reason: Deeply nested loops (7*31*31*8 iterations) caused excessive kernel complexity and slow execution (14ms vs 2.9ms reference)

attempted_but_failed.name: torch.compile on entire ModelNew
attempted_but_failed.reason: Compatibility issues between torch.compile's triton handling and ROCm Triton version caused compilation errors

notes: Key insight: torch.compile's Triton conv backend is much faster than MIOpen for this shape (1.21ms vs 2.56ms)
Post-conv operations (divide, maxpool, global avg pool, bias add, sum) only take ~0.37ms total in eager mode
Fusing all post-conv ops into one Triton kernel with nested loops is slower than separate PyTorch ops
Best approach: torch.compile on conv + simple Triton kernel for final bias add + sum

attempted_but_failed.name: trial_4
